3D printer fleet management: monitor pri: 3D printer fleet management: monitor printers, manage the print queue, track filament, and automate print jobs.
API file (hash from Upload File action): API file (hash from Upload File action)
API file (hex id from Upload File action: API file (hex id from Upload File action)
Add a new filament spool to your invento: Add a new filament spool to your inventory. Use the free-text brand/color fields for ad-hoc spools or the catalog ids for known SKUs.
Add an existing file (API-uploaded or us: Add an existing file (API-uploaded or user-file) to the print queue. For uploading a new file at the same time, use "Upload File & Add to Queue".
Alternative to File — an HTTPS URL the p: Alternative to File — an HTTPS URL the piece will stream directly to SimplyPrint without buffering the whole file in memory. Recommended for files over ~500 MB. The URL must return a Content-Length header (S3 pre-signed URLs, Dropbox raw links, etc.). If set, the File input is ignored.

Alternative to File — an HTTPS URL the piece will stream directly to SimplyPrint without buffering the whole file in memory. Recommended for files over ~500 MB. The URL must return a Content-Length header (S3 pre-signed URLs, Dropbox raw links, signed Google Drive download URLs, etc.). If set, the File input is ignored.
Alternative to grams — share of the spoo: Alternative to grams — share of the spool still on the reel.
Amount of filament still on the spool. F: Amount of filament still on the spool. For a brand-new untouched spool, set this equal to "Total length / weight". For a partially-used spool, set this lower. Same unit as "Length type". (Backend's `length_used` POST param is, despite its name, semantically "remaining" — see Create.php math.)
Approve one or more pending queue items.
Archive one or more completed print jobs: Archive one or more completed print jobs (removes them from the active history view).
Assign a filament spool to a printer at: Assign a filament spool to a printer at a specific nozzle + extruder slot. Defaults to nozzle 0 / extruder 0 (single-nozzle single-extruder printers); set the indices for multi-nozzle (e.g. IDEX, toolchanger) or multi-material (MMS, MMU) printers.
Assign one or more queue items to specif: Assign one or more queue items to specific printers, printer models, and/or printer groups. Replaces the current assignments on each item.
Attach an existing custom tag to a print: Attach an existing custom tag to a printer, printer group, file, or queue item. Use "Create or Update Tag" first to get the tag ID.
Bootstrap badge color used to render the: Bootstrap badge color used to render the tag.
Bring a completed (done) queue item back: Bring a completed (done) queue item back to the active queue.
Call any SimplyPrint REST endpoint the p: Call any SimplyPrint REST endpoint the piece does not wrap directly. Useful for admin / partner / school-dashboard endpoints and anything else behind OAuth scopes.
Cancel a print that's queued/staged on a: Cancel a print that's queued/staged on a printer but hasn't started yet (status = print_pending). Distinct from "Cancel Print", which targets the active print.
Cancel the current print on a printer. T: Cancel the current print on a printer. This is destructive — the job cannot be resumed.
Cap on returned spools (1–100). Leave em: Cap on returned spools (1–100). Leave empty for no cap. Backend has no offset-pagination on this endpoint, so this is the only cap available.
Click **Connect** below to authorize Act: Click **Connect** below to authorize Activepieces to access your SimplyPrint account. The connection refreshes automatically. Revoke any time from **SimplyPrint → Panel → Settings → Connected apps**.
Create N identical spools at once (cappe: Create N identical spools at once (capped at 500). Defaults to 1.
Create a new custom tag, or rename / re-: Create a new custom tag, or rename / re-style an existing one (pass the tag ID to edit).
Create a new folder in the user file sys: Create a new folder in the user file system, or update an existing one (pass the folder ID to edit). Optionally an "organization" / shared folder with per-rank view/upload/modify permissions.
Create or update a queue group. Pass an: Create or update a queue group. Pass an existing group ID to edit it; omit it to create a new one. Requires the Queue Groups feature on the account.
Custom-field endpoints are not yet grant: Custom-field endpoints are not yet granted to OAuth tokens. Use List Custom Fields to look up IDs.
Custom-field values applied to every sta: Custom-field values applied to every started job (PRINT_JOB). Keyed by custom-field UUID (fieldId) → value.
Delete a custom tag from the account. Th: Delete a custom tag from the account. The tag is removed from every printer/file/queue item it was attached to.
Delete a queue group. Items in the group: Delete a queue group. Items in the group are moved to "Move to" group, or to the first remaining group if not specified, or set to ungrouped if it was the last group.
Delete every item from the print queue (: Delete every item from the print queue (optionally filtered to a group or done-only). Destructive.
Delete one or more folders (and everythi: Delete one or more folders (and everything in them). All-or-nothing: if any folder isn't modifiable by the caller, the whole call fails.
Deny a pending queue item (remove or sen: Deny a pending queue item (remove or send back for revision).
Destination folder. Use 0 for the root f: Destination folder. Use 0 for the root folder.
Detach a specific filament spool from wh: Detach a specific filament spool from whichever printer it is currently assigned to.
Display name for the group (max 64 chars: Display name for the group (max 64 chars).
Download a file from SimplyPrint by user: Download a file from SimplyPrint by user-file UID, queue-item ID, or print-job UID. Returns the file as an Activepieces file reference plus content-type / size metadata.
Endpoint path relative to the account, e: Endpoint path relative to the account, e.g. "printers/Get" or "queue/AddItem". Do not include the host or the account ID segment.
Entity category the fields belong to.
Explain why the item is being denied. Sh: Explain why the item is being denied. Shown to the submitter.
Fetch a single print job's full record b: Fetch a single print job's full record by its UID — timeline, pictures, spools used, gcode analysis, filament data, cost, AI inference, custom fields, etc.
Fetch a single queue item by numeric ID: Fetch a single queue item by numeric ID — returns the formatted record (file metadata, assignments, custom fields, …).
Fetch high-level printing statistics (to: Fetch high-level printing statistics (total prints, materials used, hours, etc.).
Fetch the open notifications/events on o: Fetch the open notifications/events on one or more printers. Pairs well with the `requires_attention` bucket from "Get Farm Overview".
Field to sort the result list by. Leave: Field to sort the result list by. Leave empty to use backend default ordering.
File data (G-code, STL, 3MF). Used if \": File data (G-code, STL, 3MF). Used if "File URL" is empty. AP materializes the whole file into RAM before this action runs — for very large files prefer the File URL input below.
File extensions allowed in this group (w: File extensions allowed in this group (without the dot, e.g. `gcode`, `bgcode`). Leave empty for "any".
Filter by empty / non-empty status. \"Em: Filter by empty / non-empty status. "Empty" means filament left ≤ 0 or marked as emptied.
Fires when a balance is adjusted by an a: Fires when a balance is adjusted by an administrator.
Fires when a completed or cancelled main: Fires when a completed or cancelled maintenance job is reopened.
Fires when a completed queue item is sen: Fires when a completed queue item is sent back into the active queue.
Fires when a custom tag is attached to a: Fires when a custom tag is attached to a printer.
Fires when a custom tag is detached from: Fires when a custom tag is detached from a printer.
Fires when a filament spool is added to: Fires when a filament spool is added to the account.
Fires when a filament spool is assigned: Fires when a filament spool is assigned to a printer.
Fires when a filament spool is deleted.
Fires when a filament spool is removed f: Fires when a filament spool is removed from a printer.
Fires when a filament spool's metadata i: Fires when a filament spool's metadata is updated.
Fires when a file is added to the print: Fires when a file is added to the print queue.
Fires when a maintenance job is cancelle: Fires when a maintenance job is cancelled before completion.
Fires when a maintenance job is complete: Fires when a maintenance job is completed.
Fires when a maintenance job is created.
Fires when a maintenance job is deleted.
Fires when a maintenance job's metadata: Fires when a maintenance job's metadata is edited.
Fires when a new organization user is aw: Fires when a new organization user is awaiting admin approval.
Fires when a new user signs up for the o: Fires when a new user signs up for the organization.
Fires when a paused print is resumed.
Fires when a pending queue item is appro: Fires when a pending queue item is approved.
Fires when a pending queue item is denie: Fires when a pending queue item is denied.
Fires when a previous charge is refunded: Fires when a previous charge is refunded.
Fires when a print completes successfull: Fires when a print completes successfully.
Fires when a print is cancelled before f: Fires when a print is cancelled before finishing.
Fires when a print is paused (manually o: Fires when a print is paused (manually or by the AI failure watcher).
Fires when a print job starts on any pri: Fires when a print job starts on any printer.
Fires when a print terminates with a fai: Fires when a print terminates with a failure.
Fires when a printer hits its configured: Fires when a printer hits its configured AutoPrint cycle limit.
Fires when a printer is marked out of or: Fires when a printer is marked out of order (or back in service).
Fires when a printer's AI failure detect: Fires when a printer's AI failure detection is enabled, disabled, or reconfigured.
Fires when a printer's AutoPrint enabled: Fires when a printer's AutoPrint enabled/disabled state changes.
Fires when a printer's active nozzle dia: Fires when a printer's active nozzle diameter is updated.
Fires when a printer's bed is reported c: Fires when a printer's bed is reported cleared (ready for the next job).
Fires when a printer's loaded material i: Fires when a printer's loaded material is updated.
Fires when a queue group (or the whole q: Fires when a queue group (or the whole queue) is emptied.
Fires when a queue item is moved to anot: Fires when a queue item is moved to another group or reordered.
Fires when a queue item is removed from: Fires when a queue item is removed from the queue.
Fires when a queue item is submitted and: Fires when a queue item is submitted and awaits approval.
Fires when a quota period rolls over and: Fires when a quota period rolls over and a user's quota is reset.
Fires when a quota request is approved o: Fires when a quota request is approved or denied.
Fires when a recurring maintenance sched: Fires when a recurring maintenance schedule is created.
Fires when a recurring maintenance schedule is deleted.
Fires when a recurring maintenance schedule is edited.
Fires when a reported problem is resolve: Fires when a reported problem is resolved.
Fires when a scheduled maintenance job p: Fires when a scheduled maintenance job passes its due date without being completed.
Fires when a spare part is added to inve: Fires when a spare part is added to inventory.
Fires when a spare part is removed from: Fires when a spare part is removed from inventory.
Fires when a spare part's metadata is up: Fires when a spare part's metadata is updated.
Fires when a spare part's stock count is: Fires when a spare part's stock count is adjusted.
Fires when a spare part's stock drops be: Fires when a spare part's stock drops below its low-stock threshold.
Fires when a user balance is charged (de: Fires when a user balance is charged (debited).
Fires when a user balance is topped up (: Fires when a user balance is topped up (credited).
Fires when a user reports a problem on a: Fires when a user reports a problem on a printer.
Fires when a user submits a request for: Fires when a user submits a request for more quota.
Fires when a user's quota is manually ad: Fires when a user's quota is manually adjusted.
Fires when an AI failure alert is dismis: Fires when an AI failure alert is dismissed as a false positive.
Fires when an individual maintenance tas: Fires when an individual maintenance task is checked off.
Fires when an individual maintenance task is skipped.
Fires when one or more objects in a mult: Fires when one or more objects in a multi-object print are skipped.
Fires when the AI failure detector flags: Fires when the AI failure detector flags a potential print failure.
Fires when the account-wide AutoPrint ma: Fires when the account-wide AutoPrint master switch is toggled.
Fires when work begins on a maintenance: Fires when work begins on a maintenance job.
For multi-action notifications, the acti: For multi-action notifications, the action key to invoke. Leave empty for single-action or dismiss-only notifications.
Free-text brand name (always required, e: Free-text brand name (always required, even when catalog ids are set).
Get detailed information about a specifi: Get detailed information about a specific filament spool by numeric ID or 4-character short ID (uid).
Get detailed information about a specific printer.
Hex color (e.g. `#FF8800`). For multi-co: Hex color (e.g. `#FF8800`). For multi-color spools, comma-separate (e.g. `#FF8800,#0044CC`).
Hex id returned by Upload File. Required: Hex id returned by Upload File. Required when source is "API file".
Items per page (max 100). Defaults to 10: Items per page (max 100). Defaults to 100 when paging is requested.
Items per page (max 100). Defaults to 50: Items per page (max 100). Defaults to 50 (backend default).
Items per page (max 100). Defaults to ba: Items per page (max 100). Defaults to backend default.
Leave empty (or 0) to list root-level fi: Leave empty (or 0) to list root-level files. Use -1 for the "all files" flat view.
List all custom field definitions on you: List all custom field definitions on your account.
List all custom tags configured on your: List all custom tags configured on your account.
List completed print jobs, optionally fi: List completed print jobs, optionally filtered to a single printer.
List every printer in your SimplyPrint a: List every printer in your SimplyPrint account with current status.
List every queue group on your account.
List filament spools in your SimplyPrint: List filament spools in your SimplyPrint account, with optional filters and sort.
List files in your SimplyPrint account,: List files in your SimplyPrint account, optionally within a folder and/or filtered by a search term.
List items in the print queue, optionall: List items in the print queue, optionally filtered to a single queue group.
List queue items awaiting approval, deni: List queue items awaiting approval, denied, or sent back for revision.
Mark a printer notification as resolved: Mark a printer notification as resolved (or dismissed). For notifications with multiple actions, pass `action` to pick one or `force: true` to resolve without choosing.
Mark a printer's bed as cleared after a: Mark a printer's bed as cleared after a print finishes. Bread-and-butter step in AutoPrint flows — pair with a "print done" trigger to automate bed clearing.
Mark as dismissed rather than resolved.
Mark one or more printers as out-of-orde: Mark one or more printers as out-of-order (or clear that flag). Requires the Out-of-Order feature on the account.
Master toggle for approval workflow on t: Master toggle for approval workflow on this group.
Maximum number of jobs to return per pag: Maximum number of jobs to return per page (default 25, max 100).
Move a queue item to a different queue g: Move a queue item to a different queue group.
Move one or more user files to a differe: Move one or more user files to a different folder.
Name to store the downloaded file under: Name to store the downloaded file under in AP. Defaults to the Content-Disposition value or the identifier.
Name to store the file under on SimplyPr: Name to store the file under on SimplyPrint. MUST include the correct extension (e.g. .gcode, .stl, .3mf) — the backend picks the file type from the extension, not the contents. Required when using File URL unless the URL path ends in a filename with an extension. Optional with File (defaults to source filename).
Net grams of filament left on the spool.
New display name (max 256 chars). Keep t: New display name (max 256 chars). Keep the existing extension — backend infers file type from it.
No bed types available for your printers: No bed types available for your printers — the gcode analyzer will auto-tag on upload.
No printer models detected — add a print: No printer models detected — add a printer first.
Nozzle flow category. Leave empty to aut: Nozzle flow category. Leave empty to auto-tag from the gcode file.
Nozzle material. Leave empty to auto-tag: Nozzle material. Leave empty to auto-tag from the gcode file.
Number of copies to queue (defaults to 1: Number of copies to queue (defaults to 1).
Numeric ID (or UID string for files) of: Numeric ID (or UID string for files) of the entity to detach the tag from.
Numeric ID of the destination folder. Us: Numeric ID of the destination folder. Use 0 (or leave empty) to save to the root of the user file system. Look up folder IDs with the "List Files" action.
Numeric ID of the destination queue grou: Numeric ID of the destination queue group.
Numeric ID of the folder to edit. Omit t: Numeric ID of the folder to edit. Omit to create a new folder.
Numeric ID of the group to edit. Omit to: Numeric ID of the group to edit. Omit to create a new group.
Numeric ID of the parent folder. Use 0 f: Numeric ID of the parent folder. Use 0 for root.
Numeric ID of the queue group to delete.
Numeric IDs (or UID strings for files) o: Numeric IDs (or UID strings for files) of the entities to tag. Provide one or many.
Numeric IDs of pending queue items to ap: Numeric IDs of pending queue items to approve.
Numeric IDs of the target entities.
Numeric custom tag ID to delete.
Numeric custom tag ID to remove.
Numeric custom tag IDs to attach. From \: Numeric custom tag IDs to attach. From "List Tags" or "Create or Update Tag".
Numeric id of the material type / profil: Numeric id of the material type / profile (look up via "List Filaments" results — `type.id` on each spool — or via your filament management page). EITHER this OR a "Catalog filament ID" (+ optional "Catalog variant ID") pair is required when creating a new spool — the backend uses the catalog chain to auto-resolve / auto-import a profile. Throws "The Filament type is required" if neither is set.
Numeric indices of the objects to skip (: Numeric indices of the objects to skip (as exposed by the slicer / printer's object list).
Numeric model IDs the file is allowed to: Numeric model IDs the file is allowed to print on. Leave empty to clear model-restriction.
Numeric notification event ID. Use \"Get: Numeric notification event ID. Use "Get Printer Notifications" to look it up.
Numeric printer ID of the active print.
Numeric printer ID the notification belo: Numeric printer ID the notification belongs to.
Numeric printer ID. In automation flows: Numeric printer ID. In automation flows this typically flows in from a trigger or upstream step.
Numeric printer ID. Typically piped in f: Numeric printer ID. Typically piped in from an upstream step.
Numeric printer IDs the file is allowed: Numeric printer IDs the file is allowed to print on. Leave empty to clear printer-restriction.
Numeric printer IDs to assign to. Leave: Numeric printer IDs to assign to. Leave empty to clear printer assignments.
Numeric printer IDs whose pending prints: Numeric printer IDs whose pending prints should be cancelled.
Numeric printer group IDs. Leave empty t: Numeric printer group IDs. Leave empty to clear group assignments.
Numeric printer model IDs. Leave empty t: Numeric printer model IDs. Leave empty to clear model assignments.
Numeric queue group ID to filter by. Lea: Numeric queue group ID to filter by. Leave empty for the whole queue.
Numeric queue item ID. Typically piped i: Numeric queue item ID. Typically piped in from an upstream step.
Numeric queue item IDs to update.
Numeric spool ID, OR the 4-character sho: Numeric spool ID, OR the 4-character short ID (`uid`) printed on the QR sticker / NFC tag.
Numeric spool ID, OR the 4-character short ID (`uid`) printed on the QR sticker / NFC tag. In automation flows this typically comes from a previous step (trigger payload, MCP response, etc.).
Numeric tag ID to edit. Omit to create a: Numeric tag ID to edit. Omit to create a new tag.
Object keyed by custom-field UUID (field: Object keyed by custom-field UUID (fieldId) → value. Converted to the backend submission array automatically.
One-shot fleet summary: total printer co: One-shot fleet summary: total printer count plus per-bucket lists (online/offline, printing/paused, awaiting bed clear, in maintenance, requires-attention, AI failure detections, …). Use this instead of paginating "List Printers" when you just need counts and the printers in each state.
Only applicable for the \"print\" catego: Only applicable for the "print" category. Pick the specific entity type the fields belong to.
Only applied when "Sort by" is set.
Only return spools currently assigned to: Only return spools currently assigned to this printer (numeric ID).
Only used when \"Insert at\" is \"Specif: Only used when "Insert at" is "Specific position". 1-based index (1 = very top). Requires queue-reorder permission.
Only used when \"Shared folder\" is true: Only used when "Shared folder" is true. Numeric user rank IDs allowed to modify / delete contents.
Only used when "Shared folder" is true. Numeric user rank IDs allowed to upload to the folder.
Only used when "Shared folder" is true. Numeric user rank IDs allowed to view the folder.
Optional 1-4 star rating for the just-fi: Optional 1-4 star rating for the just-finished print.
Optional note explaining why the jobs we: Optional note explaining why the jobs were archived (max 500 chars).
Optional slicer/print-time metadata (noz: Optional slicer/print-time metadata (nozzle, filament_material, print_speed, layer_height, infill, quality). Arbitrary keys allowed.
Optional target group ID for orphaned it: Optional target group ID for orphaned items. If omitted, items go to the first remaining group (or become ungrouped if this is the last).
Optional — e.g. PRINT_QUEUE, FILE, USER. Leave empty to list all.
Optional. 4-character short id printed o: Optional. 4-character short id printed on the spool QR sticker. Auto-generated if omitted.
Optional. JSON array `[{\"id\": <queueIt: Optional. JSON array `[{"id": <queueItemId>, "value": {"<fieldId>": <value>}}]`. Per-item PRINT_JOB custom fields when starting a multi-queue job.
Optional. JSON object mapping printer id: Optional. JSON object mapping printer id → multi-material slot configuration. Leave empty for single-material printers.
Optional. Limit who can see the group ba: Optional. Limit who can see the group based on user rank IDs.
Optional. NFC tag identifier for app tra: Optional. NFC tag identifier for app tracking.
Optional. Numeric id of the storage loca: Optional. Numeric id of the storage location.
Optional. Object keyed by custom-field U: Optional. Object keyed by custom-field UUID (fieldId) → value. PRINT category / USER_FILE sub-category is auto-applied.
Optional. Object keyed by custom-field UUID (fieldId) → value. PRINT_QUEUE category is auto-applied.
Optional. Printer IDs to skip when match: Optional. Printer IDs to skip when matching (e.g. printers currently busy).
Optional. Restrict this group to specifi: Optional. Restrict this group to specific printer groups.
Optional. Restrict this group to specific printer models.
Optional. Restrict this group to specific printers.
Optional. Spool cost in account currency: Optional. Spool cost in account currency (cents/whole-units depending on your currency).
Optional. User ranks whose submissions n: Optional. User ranks whose submissions need approval.
Optional. User ranks whose submissions s: Optional. User ranks whose submissions skip approval.
Override the empty spool weight used for: Override the empty spool weight used for the gross calculation.
Page number (1-based). Only applies to t: Page number (1-based). Only applies to the active-queue path; ignored when "Include completed items" is on.
Paginated usage history for a specific s: Paginated usage history for a specific spool — print jobs, weight adjustments, drying cycles, etc.
Pause the current print on a printer.
Permanently delete one or more user file: Permanently delete one or more user files by UID. Returns a partial-success report — files the caller can't modify are reported in `errors` while accessible files are deleted.
Persist empty spool weight on spool reco: Persist empty spool weight on spool record
Pick a printer from your account.
Preview Next Queue Items per Printer: Preview Next Queue Items per Printer
Print job UID string (typically 10+ char: Print job UID string (typically 10+ characters). Comes from "List Print History" results or webhook payloads.
Printer bed surface. Leave empty to auto: Printer bed surface. Leave empty to auto-tag from the gcode file.
Read-only preview of the next-up queue i: Read-only preview of the next-up queue item for each given printer (no dedup, no offline checks). For each printer, returns either the matched queue item or the issues blocking a match. Different from "Get Next Queue Items": that one dedupes for actual scheduling, this one shows what each printer would pick.
Record that one or more spools were just: Record that one or more spools were just dried. Updates each spool's last-dried timestamp.
Remove a single custom tag from one prin: Remove a single custom tag from one printer, printer group, file, or queue item.
Remove an item from the print queue. Des: Remove an item from the print queue. Destructive — the item is gone.
Rename a user file and / or update its c: Rename a user file and / or update its compatible-printer assignments. (Thumbnail upload is not exposed here — multipart isn't supported on this endpoint over OAuth via the AP HTTP client.)
Required when source is \"API file\". He: Required when source is "API file". Hex hash returned by Upload File.
Required when source is \"User file\". T: Required when source is "User file". The UserFile.uid string.
Resolve immediately even if multiple act: Resolve immediately even if multiple actions are available (skips picking one).
Resolve the next-up queue items for one: Resolve the next-up queue items for one or more printers using the company's match criteria (with deduplication so the same item is never assigned twice). Returns the matched queue object with item references per printer.
Restore one or more archived print jobs: Restore one or more archived print jobs to the active history view.
Restrict this queue item to one or more: Restrict this queue item to one or more specific printers. Leave empty to allow any eligible printer.
Restrict to a single approval status. De: Restrict to a single approval status. Defaults to all (pending + denied + revision).
Restrict to jobs from a specific printer: Restrict to jobs from a specific printer. Leave empty for all printers.
Resume a paused print on a printer.
Return `{ body, headers, status }` inste: Return `{ body, headers, status }` instead of just the body.
Return information about the SimplyPrint: Return information about the SimplyPrint account connected to this flow.
Send raw G-code commands to an operation: Send raw G-code commands to an operational printer (requires Print Farm plan).
Set custom field values on one or more e: Set custom field values on one or more entities (queue items, files, printers, etc.).
Skip one or more individual model object: Skip one or more individual model objects on the current print (printer must be in PRINTING / PAUSED / PAUSING / CANCELLING / RESUMING state). Single printer per call.
Specific page number (1-based). Leave em: Specific page number (1-based). Leave empty to walk all pages and return every field.
Specific page number (1-based). Leave empty to walk all pages and return every printer.
Spool IDs (numeric) or 4-character short: Spool IDs (numeric) or 4-character short IDs (uid) — comma-separated short IDs are resolved one-by-one. Pure numeric values skip the lookup round-trip.
Spool size — interpretation depends on \: Spool size — interpretation depends on "Length type" below. Required when creating a new spool.
Start a print job on one or more printer: Start a print job on one or more printers from an existing file, queue item, or print-job reference.
Substring match against brand name. Leav: Substring match against brand name. Leave empty for no filter.
Substring match against color name, hex,: Substring match against color name, hex, or group. Leave empty for no filter.
Substring match against material type, e: Substring match against material type, e.g. `PLA`, `PETG`. Leave empty for no filter.
Target nozzle diameter, e.g. `0.4`. Leav: Target nozzle diameter, e.g. `0.4`. Leave empty to auto-tag from the gcode file.
This account has no custom tags — create: This account has no custom tags — create one in SimplyPrint first.
Total weight including the empty spool. Combine with "subtract empty spool" to compute net.
True = mark as out-of-order. False = cle: True = mark as out-of-order. False = clear the flag.
UID for \"User file\" / \"Print job\", n: UID for "User file" / "Print job", numeric ID for "Queue item". Look these up via "List Files" / "List Print History" / "List Queue Items".
UID of an existing user-file (from \"Lis: UID of an existing user-file (from "List Files"). Required when source is "User file". Accepts only the single file UID string.
UID string of the file to update (10-36: UID string of the file to update (10-36 chars). Look up via "List Files".
UID strings of the files to delete. Look: UID strings of the files to delete. Look up via "List Files".
Unit for \"Total length / weight\" and \: Unit for "Total length / weight" and "Length used".
Update a queue item (amount, note, etc.): Update a queue item (amount, note, etc.).
Update the remaining filament on a spool: Update the remaining filament on a spool. Provide ONE of "grams remaining" / "percent remaining" / "weighed gross" — the other two derive from it.
Upload a file (or HTTPS URL) and save it: Upload a file (or HTTPS URL) and save it to your SimplyPrint user file system, optionally inside a specific folder. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically.
Upload a file or HTTPS URL to SimplyPrin: Upload a file or HTTPS URL to SimplyPrint. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically. Returns the API file id — pass it to "Add File to Queue" or "Start Print" later.
Upload a local file or HTTPS URL to Simp: Upload a local file or HTTPS URL to SimplyPrint and add it to the print queue in one step. URL uploads stream with ~95 MiB peak RAM; File uploads are buffered by AP. Files over 95 MiB are chunked automatically. For starting the print afterwards, chain a "Start Print" action.
User-file UID string. Comma-separate to: User-file UID string. Comma-separate to move several files at once. Use "List Files" to look up UIDs.
Was the bed actually cleared (true) or d: Was the bed actually cleared (true) or did it fail (false)? Drives AutoPrint clear-count logic.
What kind of entity to tag.
When a search term is provided, search a: When a search term is provided, search across every folder instead of just the current one.
When checked, only done items are remove: When checked, only done items are removed. Pending / printing / failed items stay.
When checked, resets the AutoPrint clear: When checked, resets the AutoPrint cleared-bed counter on the printer.
When checked, the submitter can edit and: When checked, the submitter can edit and resubmit. Otherwise the item is removed.
When drying completed. Defaults to now i: When drying completed. Defaults to now if omitted (e.g. `2026-04-26T12:34:00Z`).
When true, also returns the schema for t: When true, also returns the schema for the custom fields attached to the job (vs just values).
When true, deletes any thumbnail current: When true, deletes any thumbnail currently stored on the file.
When true, no real queue items can be ad: When true, no real queue items can be added — used for placeholder/grouping-only groups.
When true, replaces the subject's curren: When true, replaces the subject's current custom tags. When false (default), merges with existing tags.
When true, the folder is shared across t: When true, the folder is shared across the company per the rank-based permissions below. Requires the Shared Folders feature.
Where in the queue to place the new item: Where in the queue to place the new item.
Whether to include only assigned spools,: Whether to include only assigned spools, only unassigned, or both.
Which extruder on the chosen nozzle (0-b: Which extruder on the chosen nozzle (0-based). Use 0 for single-extruder nozzles. Multi-material setups (MMS, MMU, AMS) use 1+ for the additional extruders feeding the same nozzle. Backend rejects with "Extruder out of range" if the nozzle doesn't have this many extruders.
Which kind of existing reference to star: Which kind of existing reference to start from.
Which kind of file to fetch.
Which nozzle on the printer (0-based). U: Which nozzle on the printer (0-based). Use 0 for single-nozzle printers. Multi-nozzle printers (IDEX, toolchanger) use 1+ for the additional nozzles. Backend rejects with "Nozzle out of range" if the printer doesn't have this many nozzles.
Which queue group to add the item to. Re: Which queue group to add the item to. Required if your account has queue groups configured. Leave empty only if no queue groups exist.
